Chapter:
Affine actions
Let G and H be groups, let H act on G (via automorphisms) by \alpha \colon H \to \operatorname{Aut}(G) \colon h \mapsto \alpha_h and let \delta \colon H \to G be a group derivation with respect to this action. Then we can construct a new action, called the affine action associated to \delta, by G \times H \to G \colon g^h = \alpha_h(g) \delta(h). If K is a subgroup of H, then the restriction of the affine action of H on G to K coincides with the affine action of K on G associated to the restriction of \delta to K.
Algorithms designed for computing with twisted conjugacy classes can be leveraged to do computations involving affine actions, see for a description on this.
Please note that the functions in this chapter require G and H to either both be finite, or both be PcpGroups.
Creating an affine action
the affine action of K associated to the derivation der.
The group K must be a subgroup of Source(der).
gap> aff := AffineActionByGroupDerivation( H, der ); function( g, k ) ... end
Operations for affine actions
These functions are analogues of existing &GAP; functions for group actions.
OrbitAffineAction
the orbit of g under the affine action of K associated to der.
The group K must be a subgroup of Source(der).
OrbitsAffineAction
a list containing the orbits under the affine action of K associated to der if there are finitely many, or fail if there are infinitely many.
The group K must be a subgroup of Source(der).
NrOrbitsAffineAction
the number of orbits under the affine action of K associated to der.
StabiliserAffineAction
the stabiliser of g under the affine action of K associated to der.
The group K must be a subgroup of Source(der).
RepresentativeAffineAction
an element of K that maps g1 to g2 under the affine action of K associated to der, or fail if no such element exists.
The group K must be a subgroup of Source(der).
gap> g1 := G.1;; gap> orb := OrbitAffineAction( H, g1, der ); f1^G gap> NrOrbitsAffineAction( H, der ); 10 gap> stab := StabiliserAffineAction( H, g1, der );; gap> Set( stab ); [ <identity> of ..., f3, f3^2, f2^2*f5, f2*f4*f5, f2^2*f3*f5, f2*f3*f4*f5, f2^2*f3^2*f5, f2*f3^2*f4*f5 ] gap> g2 := G.1*G.4*G.5;; gap> h := RepresentativeAffineAction( H, g1, g2, der );; gap> aff( g1, h ) = g2; true
Operations on orbits of affine actions
Representative
the group element that was used to construct orb.
ActingDomain
the group whose affine action orb is an orbit of.
FunctionAction
the affine action that orb is an orbit of.
\in
true if elm is an element of orb, otherwise false.
Size
the number of elements in orb.
StabiliserOfExternalSet
the stabiliser of Representative(orb) under the action FunctionAction(orb).
List
a list containing the elements of orb.
If orb is infinite, this will run forever. It is recommended to first test the finiteness of orb using .
Random
a random element in orb.
\=
true if orb1 is equal to orb2, otherwise false.
gap> g2 in orb; true gap> G.2 in orb; false gap> Size( orb ); 8
